title: Schmaltzia schiedeana
taxonomy: I was unable to locate any information regarding the classification of the Schmaltzia schiedeana.
uses: Schmaltzia schiedeana, also known as the Mexican satinwood, has been traditionally used in folk medicine in Mexico. Its medicinal uses include treating various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The plant's bark, leaves, and roots are used to prepare remedies. The bark is believed to have anti-inflammatory properties, while the leaves are used to treat fever and skin infections. Further research is needed to confirm the efficacy of Schmaltzia schiedeana as a medicinal plant.
properties: Schmaltzia schiedeana, a plant from the Burseraceae family, has been traditionally used in Mexican and Central American folk medicine for its various therapeutic properties. Its extracts have been found to possess antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant activities. The plant is used to treat various health issues, including wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory problems. Additionally, its oil has been reported to have analgesic and anti-spasmodic effects, making it a potential remedy for pain relief. Further research is needed to fully understand the therapeutic potential of Schmaltzia schiedeana.

preparations: Schmaltzia schiedeana is a plant species that has been used in traditional medicine, particularly in Mexico. Herbal preparations from this plant are used to treat various ailments, including fever, colds, and respiratory issues. The plant's leaves and stems are typically harvested and dried for use in teas, infusions, and poultices.
